families.label: Compatibility retirement
families.objective: Retire workforce-era compatibility dependencies from live first-class UI and API surfaces.
exit_criteria: Active first-class UI no longer depends on workforce-era compatibility APIs except explicit legacy shells.

families.label: Durable persistence activation
families.objective: Activate real Postgres-backed LangGraph persistence and retire fallback-memory posture where Postgres is configured.
exit_criteria: Runtime health no longer reports fallback persistence when Postgres is configured.

families.label: Operator surface canonicalization
families.objective: Keep shell, nav, and first-class pages aligned on canonical operator surfaces.
exit_criteria: Live shell, nav, and first-class pages point to canonical operator surfaces only.

families.label: Foundry completion
families.objective: Make packet-backed foundry execution the primary build path for at least one real project.
exit_criteria: At least one real project completes packet -> slice -> run -> candidate -> promote or rollback.

families.label: Governance rehearsal
families.objective: Record live evidence for constrained, degraded, recovery, restore, rollback, and blocked-approval drills.
exit_criteria: Governance drills all have live evidence instead of prose-only claims.

families.label: Provider repair preflight
families.objective: Keep remaining provider/auth gaps explicit as blockers and repair packets rather than vague follow-up prose.
exit_criteria: Remaining provider issues are explicit blockers or repair packets.

families.label: Takeover promotion check
families.objective: Evaluate readiness for explicit promotion of the internal Athanor builder stack.
exit_criteria: All takeover criteria are green and evidence-backed, but promotion remains explicit.

programs.label: Launch readiness bootstrap
programs.objective: Use the external recursive builder stack to finish the Athanor builder stack until explicit takeover is justified.

seed_slices.objective: Finish retiring workforce-era compatibility readers from live first-class UI and API surfaces.
Activate real Postgres-backed persistence and remove fallback posture where Postgres is configured.
Keep the live shell, route family, and command-center pages aligned on canonical operator surfaces.
Drive at least one real project through the foundry packet path end to end.
Capture live evidence for constrained, degraded, recovery, restore, rollback, and blocked-approval drills.
Keep provider auth blockers and repair packets explicit and evidence-backed.
Evaluate when the internal Athanor builder stack is ready for explicit takeover promotion.
